Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple A sophisticated attack targeting healthcare infrastructure, leveraging remote code execution vulnerabilities in medical devices to disrupt critical systems like HVAC and pharmaceutical storage.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disruption of critical healthcare operations and potential data exfiltration

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.technique_name: Exploitation of Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.kev: CVE-2019-1129 [CWE-XXX]: Suggested exploitation of medical device interfaces
stage.capec: CAPEC-187: Malicious Automated Software Update via Redirection
stage.description: Threat actors exploited a known RCE vulnerability in external-facing medical devices (e.g., MRI/CT scanners) to inject malicious payloads, bypassing authentication mechanisms and gaining unauthorized access to the device's internal systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.kev: CVE-2023-38831 [CWE-XXX]: Exploited scripting APIs for payload deployment
stage.description: Once inside the network, attackers deployed the FrostyGoop backdoor using PowerShell scripts, leveraging compromised web servers to execute arbitrary commands and establish persistence across the healthcare organization’s infrastructure.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Attackers manipulated environmental control systems (e.g., HVAC) by altering API parameters, causing unsafe temperature fluctuations in ICUs and disrupting pharmaceutical cold-chain storage through tampered configuration settings.

Scenario.summary: The FrostyGoop Incident involved a simple yet impactful attack chain targeting healthcare systems, beginning with RCE exploitation of medical devices, followed by PowerShell-based persistence, and culminating in the sabotage of environmental controls to cripple critical infrastructure.
